title: skills::load_skills hard-codes relative ".openclaudia/skills" — silently picks up working-dir-relative skill files
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/skills.rs:62-77
## Violation
The project-skills directory is computed as a relative PathBuf, so its meaning shifts with whatever directory the process happens to be in when load_skills() fires. Quoted lines:

    // Project skills: .openclaudia/skills/
    let project_dir = PathBuf::from(".openclaudia/skills");
    if project_dir.exists() {
        dirs_to_scan.push(project_dir);
    }

If a user runs `oc` from an unrelated directory that happens to contain a `.openclaudia/skills/` (cloned third-party repo, demo bundle), those skills load with the same privileges as the user's own. Worse, the prompt body is later injected directly into the model context via `SlashCommandResult::Skill(skill.prompt)` (slash.rs:531-534), so a hostile cwd silently steers the agent.
## Mandated refactor
Resolve project_dir against an explicit project root: walk upward from cwd looking for `.openclaudia/config.yaml` (use the same logic the config loader uses, or extract it into a shared `project_root()` helper). If no root is found, skip the project-skills load entirely. Log the resolved absolute path at `info!` so operators can verify the source. Add a startup audit that prints all skills paths the next REPL render will use.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
